title: Quiz: Vibration Isolation
description: Test your understanding of vibration sources, coupling mechanisms, and isolation techniques in quantum computing cryostats

questions.question: What is the primary source of mechanical vibration in a dry dilution refrigerator?

options.text: The turbo pump on the vacuum system
The pulse tube cooler operating at ~1.4 Hz
The 3He circulation pump
Thermal contraction during cooldown

questions.explanation: The pulse tube cooler is the dominant vibration source in dry dilution refrigerators, operating at approximately 1.4 Hz with amplitude of 10-100 micrometers at the cold head. This periodic motion must be mechanically decoupled from the sample stage.

questions.question: How can mechanical vibrations couple to superconducting qubit decoherence?

options.text: Only through direct physical impact on the chip
Through magnetic field modulation, strain effects, and cable motion
Vibrations cannot affect qubits because they are quantum systems
Only through heating of the mixing chamber

questions.explanation: Vibrations couple to qubits through multiple mechanisms: magnetic field fluctuations from moving magnetic components, mechanical strain in the substrate affecting junction properties, and cable motion introducing electrical noise. All of these can cause frequency shifts and dephasing.

questions.question: A mass-spring vibration isolator has a natural frequency of 2 Hz. At what frequency does isolation begin?

options.text: Immediately at 0 Hz
At approximately sqrt(2) times the natural frequency, ~2.8 Hz
At exactly 2 Hz
At 10x the natural frequency, 20 Hz

questions.explanation: For a simple mass-spring isolator, transmissibility equals 1 at DC and peaks at the natural frequency. Isolation (T < 1) begins at sqrt(2) times the natural frequency. For f0 = 2 Hz, isolation begins around 2.8 Hz.

questions.question: What is the purpose of flexible metal bellows in the dilution refrigerator design?

options.text: To store liquid helium
To mechanically decouple the pulse tube from the dilution unit while maintaining thermal contact
To filter electrical noise from the pulse tube
To measure the pressure in the vacuum system

questions.explanation: Flexible bellows provide mechanical isolation between the vibrating pulse tube cold stages and the dilution unit where the qubits reside. They allow thermal contact (heat can flow through the bellows walls) while acting as a low-pass mechanical filter that attenuates vibration transmission.

questions.question: An isolation system with a lower natural frequency provides better vibration isolation at the pulse tube frequency (1.4 Hz).

options.text: True
False

questions.explanation: True! Lower natural frequency means isolation begins at a lower frequency. For isolating 1.4 Hz pulse tube vibrations, you need a system with natural frequency well below 1 Hz (around 0.5 Hz or less). This is challenging but provides much better isolation at the problematic frequency.

questions.question: Which of the following are effective strategies for reducing vibration-induced qubit decoherence? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Using soft cable supports and strain relief
Mounting the cryostat on a massive, isolated foundation
Increasing the pulse tube operating frequency
Using non-magnetic materials near the sample stage

questions.explanation: Soft cable supports (a) reduce cable motion coupling, massive isolated foundations (b) provide passive seismic isolation, and non-magnetic materials (d) reduce magnetic field modulation from vibrating components. Increasing pulse tube frequency (c) would not help as the issue is the vibration amplitude, not specifically its frequency.
